title: Artifact Analysis Lab: Mask & Weapon Origins
description: Students enter a virtual archaeology analysis lab where a Venetian mask and a dungeon war karma weapon are displayed on examination pedestals. They must interact with each artifact by rotating, zooming, tagging cultural origin points, and comparing the two artifacts to complete a guided educational worksheet about cross-cultural craftsmanship.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that implements an interactive 3D archaeology analysis lab. Load both GLB models using GLTFLoader: venice_mask.glb placed on a left pedestal and dungeon_warkarma.glb on a right pedestal. Scene setup: Use a neutral museum-style environment with a dark gray floor plane and soft ambient light (intensity 0.6) plus two directional lights angled from above-left and above-right (intensity 0.8 each) to simulate gallery lighting. Set the camera at a comfortable viewing distance centered between both pedestals. Interaction steps: (1) Click an artifact to select it — the selected artifact glows with an emissive outline or highlight color and a floating HTML label appears showing its name. (2) Use mouse drag on the selected artifact to rotate it freely on its pedestal for inspection (implement raycasting for selection and per-object OrbitControls-style rotation). (3) Click a 'Tag Origin' button in an HTML overlay panel to place a colored annotation sphere on the artifact's surface at the clicked point, with a text input popup to label it (e.g., 'Gilding technique' or 'Forged blade edge'). Allow up to 3 tags per artifact. (4) Click a 'Compare' button that animates both artifacts to the center, side by side, slowly auto-rotating so the student can visually compare them. (5) A 'Quiz' button reveals a simple 2-question multiple choice overlay asking which artifact is European and which is fantasy-medieval, checking answers with green/red feedback. Display a progress tracker at the top showing completed steps (Selected, Rotated, Tagged, Compared, Quizzed) as a horizontal step bar that fills in as the user completes each action. Use OrbitControls for general camera navigation but disable it during artifact-specific rotation. Ensure responsive layout and add a brief instruction tooltip on load that fades after 4 seconds.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  selectedArtifactIndex: number,         // index of currently selected artifact (0=mask, 1=weapon, -1=none)
  maskRotationY: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of the mask in radians
  weaponRotationY: number,         // current Y-axis rotation of the weapon in radians
  maskTagCount: number,         // number of origin tags placed on the mask (0-3)
  weaponTagCount: number,         // number of origin tags placed on the weapon (0-3)
  totalTagsPlaced: number,         // total tags placed across both artifacts
  isTagModeActive: boolean,         // whether tag placement mode is currently enabled
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance of camera from scene center in units
  maskEmissiveIntensity: number,         // emissive glow intensity for mask when selected (0-1)
  weaponEmissiveIntensity: number,         // emissive glow intensity for weapon when selected (0-1)
  lastInteractionTimestamp: number,         // timestamp of last user interaction in milliseconds
}
